title: ONGC Geology — Advanced Mock 14 — Conceptual
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a rift basin setting, the timing of peak oil generation relative to trap formation is controlled primarily by:
options: The instantaneous heating rate of the syn-rift source kitchen
The intersection of the source rock's kinetic window with the burial–thermal history path
The clay mineralogy of the overlying seal unit
The lateral continuity of the carrier bed above the source
questions.solution: Peak oil generation occurs when the source rock reaches its kinetic maturity window (Tmax of the principal kerogen reaction). In rift basins, the critical exploration risk is whether this kinetic window was reached before, during, or after structural trap formation. This is assessed by integrating burial history modelling with programmed pyrolysis kinetic parameters (Ea, A-factor) for the specific kerogen type.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A Class III AVO anomaly at a gas–sand interface implies that the reflection coefficient at near-offsets is:
options: Strongly positive and decreases with offset
Weakly negative and becomes more negative with offset
Near zero and remains constant with offset
Positive at near-offsets, crosses zero, then becomes negative
questions.solution: In the Rutherford & Williams (1989) AVO classification, a Class III anomaly occurs when the gas sand has lower impedance than the overlying shale (negative intercept, negative gradient). At near-offsets the reflection is weakly negative, and with increasing angle of incidence it becomes more strongly negative — the classic bright spot response.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a classic Bouma sequence, the division most indicative of sustained high-density turbidity currents and which is absent in low-density turbidites is:
questions.solution: The Ta division (massive or normally graded structureless sandstone) is deposited from high-density turbidity currents (HDC) by mechanisms including grain flow, fluidized flow, and deposition from a dense basal layer. It requires sediment concentrations exceeding ~20–30 vol%. Low-density turbidity currents produce only divisions Tb–Te.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a thrust-faulting stress regime (σH > σv > σh), wellbore breakouts in a vertical well will preferentially develop:
questions.solution: Wellbore breakouts form at the point of maximum compressive stress concentration around the wellbore wall. In a vertical well under a thrust-faulting regime, the hoop stress is maximised at the borehole wall aligned with σh.

media.caption: Stress distribution around a vertical wellbore in thrust-faulting regime

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of C₃₀ hopane to C₃₀ moretane decreases with increasing thermal maturity because:
options: Moretane is thermally more stable than hopane at high temperatures
Hopane is kinetically favoured in diagenesis while moretane is the product of catagenesis
The 17β(H),21α(H) configuration is thermodynamically less stable and isomerises to the 17α(H),21β(H) configuration
Bacterial input increases the moretane concentration relative to hopane
questions.solution: The 17β(H),21α(H)-hopane (moretane) configuration is thermodynamically less stable than the 17α(H),21β(H)-hopane configuration. With increasing thermal maturity, moretane isomerises to the more stable αβ-hopane configuration. This parameter is useful for assessing thermal maturity in the early to main oil window.

media.caption: Hopane/moretane isomerisation with increasing maturity

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a passive margin setting, a maximum regressive surface (MRS) is identified on seismic sections by:
options: A downlap surface overlain by prograding clinoforms
An onlap surface at the base of a transgressive systems tract
The transition from progradation (forced or normal regression) to retrogradation (transgression)
A regional erosional unconformity with deep incised valleys
questions.solution: The maximum regressive surface (MRS) marks the change from regression (progradation/forced regression) to transgression (retrogradation). On seismic, it is identified where the depositional dip changes from progradational to retrogradational.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In NMR logging, the T₂ cutoff for distinguishing bound fluid from free fluid in carbonate reservoirs is typically set at:
questions.solution: While 33 ms is the standard T₂ cutoff for sandstone reservoirs, carbonate reservoirs require a higher cutoff of approximately 92 ms because carbonate pore systems often have larger intercrystalline and vuggy pores where shorter T₂ values may still represent bound fluid.

media.caption: NMR T₂ distribution for sandstone vs carbonate — different cutoffs

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a folded reservoir, the orientation of maximum horizontal compressive stress (σH) in the crest of an anticline is typically:
options: Parallel to the fold hinge line
Perpendicular to the fold hinge line
At 45° to the fold hinge line
questions.solution: At the crest of a doubly-plunging anticline, the outer-arc extension is oriented perpendicular to the hinge, creating a local stress perturbation where σH aligns approximately parallel to the hinge line. This controls the orientation of tension fractures that open perpendicular to the hinge.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In carbonates, the cementation exponent 'm' in Archie's first equation F = a/φᵐ is typically higher than in sandstones because:
options: Carbonates have higher density than sandstones
Carbonates often have vuggy and mouldic porosity that creates tortuous, poorly connected pore networks
Carbonates contain more clay minerals than sandstones
Archie's equation is not applicable to carbonates
questions.solution: In carbonates, 'm' commonly ranges from 2.0 to 4.0+ because vuggy, mouldic, and intercrystalline pore systems create highly tortuous electrical paths. Dissolution vugs are often isolated, requiring higher current path lengths. In Mumbai High limestone reservoirs, 'm' values of 2.5–3.5 have been reported.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The concept of 'charge risk' in petroleum exploration integrates which critical elements?
options: Reservoir quality, seal capacity, and trap geometry
Source rock presence, maturity, migration pathways, and timing relative to trap formation
Seismic amplitude, AVO response, and frequency content
Well completion design, stimulation technique, and production rate
questions.solution: Charge risk encompasses: (1) presence and quality of source rock, (2) thermal maturity, (3) migration pathways, and (4) timing (generation post-dates trap formation). In the Deccan Traps-covered areas, charge risk is particularly complex due to volcanic intrusions perturbing the thermal regime.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a water-wet reservoir, the relative permeability to oil at irreducible water saturation (S_wi) is typically:
options: Significantly less than 1.0 because the immobile water occupies pore throats and reduces effective flow area
Greater than 1.0 due to film flow of oil
Zero, because oil cannot flow at irreducible water saturation
questions.solution: At S_wi, the water phase is immobile but still occupies pore space and narrows effective pore throats, reducing the effective permeability to oil below the absolute permeability. The relative permeability to oil at S_wi is typically 0.6–0.9 in water-wet systems.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rajmahal Traps of eastern India are geochemically linked to which mantle plume event?
questions.solution: The Rajmahal Traps are linked to the Kerguelen hotspot plume activity during the Early Cretaceous (~118–120 Ma), occurring during the breakup of eastern Gondwana. This is distinct from the Deccan Traps (Réunion plume, ~65 Ma).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: On a log-log plot of pressure derivative versus time during a drawdown test, a horizontal derivative plateau indicates:
options: Linear flow from a hydraulic fracture
questions.solution: A horizontal plateau on the pressure derivative on a log-log diagnostic plot is the hallmark of infinite-acting radial flow (IARF). During IARF, the derivative is constant at qBμ/(4πkh), allowing direct calculation of formation permeability.

media.caption: Log-log pressure derivative plot showing IARF plateau

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a lowstand systems tract (LST), which sub-environment is most likely to host the best reservoir quality sands in a deepwater setting?
options: Mass transport deposits (MTD) at the base of the slope
Hemipelagic drapes in the distal basin plain
questions.solution: Basin-floor fans within the LST consist of unconfined sheet-like turbidite sands deposited at the base of the slope. These sands tend to be well-sorted, laterally extensive, and have good porosity–permeability characteristics.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The transformation of smectite to illite in shale during burial diagenesis releases silica. The reaction requires which essential ion from the pore fluid?
questions.solution: The smectite-to-illite reaction is: Smectite + K⁺ → Illite + Quartz + H₂O. Potassium is the essential reactant ion, typically sourced from K-feldspar dissolution. The released silica precipitates as quartz overgrowths, reducing sandstone porosity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A residual gravity anomaly over a salt dome in a sedimentary basin will typically show:
options: A positive anomaly due to high density of salt
A negative anomaly because salt is less dense than surrounding compacted sediments
No anomaly because salt has the same density as surrounding sediments
A positive anomaly at the surface transitioning to negative at depth
questions.solution: Rock salt (ρ ≈ 2.15–2.20 g/cm³) is significantly less dense than surrounding compacted sedimentary rocks (ρ ≈ 2.4–2.8 g/cm³). This density contrast produces a negative residual gravity anomaly.

media.caption: Negative gravity anomaly over salt diapir

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'production type curve' for a shale gas well exhibits a characteristic decline pattern best described by:
options: Hyperbolic decline transitioning to exponential decline at late time
Constant rate production followed by abrupt decline
questions.solution: Shale gas wells characteristically exhibit hyperbolic decline (b-value typically 1.0–2.0) during early and mid-life production, driven by transient linear flow. At late time, the decline transitions to exponential (b ≈ 0) as the drainage area becomes bounded.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In Indian offshore wells, the first occurrence datum (FOD) of the nannofossil Tranolithus oracus is used to define which geological age?
questions.solution: Tranolithus oracus FOD defines the base of the Campanian stage (~83.6 Ma). In Indian offshore biostratigraphy, this is a key marker for dating the Late Cretaceous succession below the Deccan Trap basalt in the Western Offshore.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In geostatistical reservoir modelling, the variogram range represents:
options: The maximum value of the regionalised variable
The distance beyond which spatial correlation between sample points becomes negligible
The minimum distance between two wellbores
The standard deviation of the property distribution
questions.solution: The variogram range is the lag distance at which the semivariogram reaches the sill (total variance), indicating that beyond this distance, spatial correlation between data points is negligible.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A thermogenic gas will typically show which relationship between δ¹³C of methane and ethane?
options: Ethane significantly more ¹³C-depleted than methane
A smaller ¹³C fractionation between methane and ethane (δ¹³C₂ − δ¹³C₁ < ~10‰)
Identical δ¹³C values for methane and ethane
Methane more ¹³C-depleted than ethane with difference exceeding 20‰
questions.solution: In thermogenic gas, both methane and ethane are generated from the same kerogen cracking process, and the ¹³C fractionation between them is relatively small. In biogenic gas, methane is strongly ¹³C-depleted while trace ethane retains a thermogenic signature, creating a large δ¹³C difference.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In seismic data processing, the primary purpose of deconvolution is to:
options: Remove random noise from the seismic trace
Compress the source wavelet and improve temporal resolution by removing the effects of the source signature
Migrate dipping reflecters to their true subsurface positions
Compensate for amplitude losses due to spherical spreading
questions.solution: Deconvolution is designed to 'undo' the convolutional effect of the source wavelet and earth filtering. By compressing the wavelet, it improves temporal resolution, removes multiples, and enhances the interpretability of thin beds.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Volcaniclastic sandstones often exhibit anomalous porosity–depth trends because:
options: Volcanic fragments are more resistant to compaction than quartz grains
Dissolution of unstable volcanic lithic fragments and feldspars creates secondary porosity at depths where normal compaction would reduce porosity
Volcaniclastic sands have lower initial porosity than quartz sands
Volcanic fragments promote early cementation, preventing any porosity loss
questions.solution: Unstable volcanic lithic fragments and feldspars are susceptible to dissolution by organic acids and CO₂ generated during kerogen maturation, creating secondary porosity at burial depths of 2–4 km.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'hard–soft water mixing line' on a Na vs Cl⁻ plot indicates:
options: Mixing of meteoric water with basinal brines, with subsequent ion exchange altering the Na/Cl ratio
Dolomitisation of limestone by Mg-rich brines
Reduction of sulfate by bacterial activity
questions.solution: When meteoric water mixes with deep basinal brines, the mixing line on a Na vs Cl⁻ cross-plot deviates from the seawater evaporation trend. Subsequent ion exchange further modifies the Na/Cl ratio. Understanding formation water chemistry is essential for identifying hydrodynamic trapping and seal integrity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Cambay Basin's half-graben geometry with a major western boundary fault produces which characteristic petroleum system pattern?
options: Source kitchen in the east, reservoirs in the west
Source kitchen along the deep western half-graben, with migration updip toward the eastern flexural flank
Random distribution of hydrocarbons with no structural control
Source and reservoir entirely within the volcanic sequence
questions.solution: The Cambay Basin's asymmetric half-graben geometry places the source kitchen in the western graben. Hydrocarbons migrate laterally updip toward the gently dipping eastern flank, where structural and stratigraphic traps are found.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Waxman–Smits equation for water saturation correction accounts for which additional conductive mechanism?
options: Cation exchange capacity (CEC) of clay minerals providing an additional conductive pathway
Conductivity of organic matter (kerogen) in shale reservoirs
questions.solution: The Waxman–Smits model modifies Archie's equation by adding a clay conductivity term (BQᵥ) to account for the cation exchange capacity of clay minerals. Clay-bound water contains exchangeable cations providing an additional conductive pathway.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Barrovian metamorphic sequence is indicative of which type of geothermal gradient?
options: High geothermal gradient (>40°C/km) — low pressure facies series
Moderate geothermal gradient (~25–35°C/km) — medium pressure facies series
Low geothermal gradient (<15°C/km) — high pressure facies series
questions.solution: The Barrovian sequence represents medium-pressure regional metamorphism with a moderate geothermal gradient (~25–35°C/km). This contrasts with the Buchan (high T, low P) and Blueschist (low T, high P) facies series.

media.caption: P–T diagram showing Barrovian, Buchan, and Blueschist facies series

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In drilling through salt-bearing formations, the primary risk of using freshwater-based mud is:
options: Loss of circulation into high-permeability salt zones
Salt dissolution creating washouts and instability, requiring saturated salt mud or oil-based mud
Excessive bit balling from salt precipitation
Hydrogen sulfide generation from salt–water interaction
questions.solution: Halite is soluble in freshwater and low-salinity muds, causing uncontrolled dissolution, washouts, and wellbore instability. The solution is saturated salt mud or oil-based mud to prevent dissolution.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In ONGC's exploration in northeast India, lineament analysis using satellite imagery is primarily used to:
options: Determine the exact depth of hydrocarbon-bearing formations
Map structural lineaments, fracture zones, and their relationship to subsurface petroleum traps
Measure the temperature of surface oil seeps
Determine the age of rock formations from space
questions.solution: Satellite-based lineament analysis maps surface expressions of deeper structural features — faults, fractures, folds — that may control subsurface petroleum traps. In northeast India, dense vegetation and difficult terrain make this a cost-effective first-pass structural mapping tool.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Immiscible gas injection for EOR is most effective when:
options: The reservoir is at bubble point pressure and the gas has high solubility in oil
The reservoir pressure is maintained above MMP and the gas relies on gravity drainage and swelling
The oil API gravity is less than 15°
questions.solution: Immiscible gas injection operates below MMP. The displacement mechanism relies on oil swelling, viscosity reduction, gravity-stable displacement, and vaporisation of light ends. It is most effective in deep, high-pressure reservoirs.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The GSSP for the base of the Neogene (base of Miocene) is defined near which location?
questions.solution: The base of the Miocene (~23.03 Ma) is defined by the GSSP at Lemkenberg/Nobbio section in the Aquitanian Alps. The Miocene succession is critical for ONGC's Bombay High and Mumbai Offshore exploration.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum hydrocarbon column height that a caprock seal can retain is controlled by:
options: The thickness of the seal only
The capillary entry pressure of the seal lithology, its thickness, and the density contrast between hydrocarbon and formation water
The porosity of the reservoir rock beneath the seal
The depositional environment of the reservoir
questions.solution: Seal capacity is governed by capillary mechanics: Pc = 2γ cos θ / r. A seal retains hydrocarbons up to the height where buoyancy pressure equals capillary entry pressure.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The ⁴⁰Ar/³⁹Ar dating method is preferred over conventional K–Ar dating because:
options: It can detect excess argon and provides step-heating age spectra that reveal thermal disturbance or argon loss events
It has a much wider applicable age range
It does not require potassium-bearing minerals
questions.solution: The ⁴⁰Ar/³⁹Ar method performs step-heating to release argon incrementally, producing an age spectrum that reveals excess argon, argon loss, and true crystallisation age. In dating the Deccan Trap basalts, this has been instrumental in resolving eruption chronology.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a restricted marine evaporite basin, the vertical facies succession from bottom to top follows the 'shrinking basin' model as:
options: Limestone → dolomite → anhydrite → halite → potash salts
questions.solution: The 'shrinking basin' model predicts: carbonate (limestone/dolomite) → sulfate (anhydrite/gypsum) → chloride (halite) → potassium/magnesium salts. This reflects progressive removal of less-soluble salts first.

media.caption: Evaporite basin — shrinking basin facies succession
media.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 400 320' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='400' height='320' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='200' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Shrinking Basin Evaporite Model</text><rect x='60' y='40' width='280' height='40' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#92400e' rx='3'/><text x='200' y='65' font-size='10' fill='#92400e' text-anchor='middle'>Potash salts (carnallite, sylvite)</text><rect x='60' y='85' width='280' height='40' fill='#fecaca' stroke='#dc2626' rx='3'/><text x='200' y='110' font-size='10' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>Halite (NaCl)</text><rect x='60' y='130' width='280' height='40' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#2563eb' rx='3'/><text x='200' y='155' font-size='10' fill='#1e40af' text-anchor='middle'>Anhydrite / Gypsum (CaSO₄)</text><rect x='60' y='175' width='280' height='40' fill='#bbf7d0' stroke='#16a34a' rx='3'/><text x='200' y='200' font-size='10' fill='#166534' text-anchor='middle'>Dolomite</text><rect x='60' y='220' width='280' height='40' fill='#e2e8f0' stroke='#64748b' rx='3'/><text x='200' y='245' font-size='10' fill='#64748b' text-anchor='middle'>Limestone</text><text x='350' y='60' font-size='8' fill='#64748b'>Most soluble</text><text x='350' y='245' font-size='8' fill='#64748b'>Least soluble</text><text x='200' y='290' font-size='10' fill='#64748b' text-anchor='middle'>↑ Increasing salinity / concentration</text><text x='200' y='310' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>Evaporation removes water → basin shrinks</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In wireline log correlation across the Mumbai High field, the 'L-II' limestone reservoir is characterised on the Gamma Ray log by:
options: High gamma ray due to high clay content
Low gamma ray (clean carbonate) with intervening high gamma ray shale breaks used as correlation markers
Uniformly moderate gamma ray with no distinguishing features
Very high potassium feldspar content giving elevated gamma ray
questions.solution: The L-II limestone is a clean carbonate (low GR) with thin shale intercalations (high GR spikes) that serve as key correlation markers. Clean limestone shows GR <20 API, shale markers show GR >80 API.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Contourite drift deposits are distinguished from turbidites by:
options: Higher clay content and always non-reservoir
Bioturbated, well-sorted, internally laminated fabrics with good lateral continuity, potentially forming stratigraphic traps
questions.solution: Contourite drift deposits are reworked by persistent bottom currents, producing well-sorted, mature sandstones with good lateral continuity. Their internal structure shows combined current and bioturbation features.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a deeply buried sandstone reservoir (>3 km), the dominant porosity-destroying mechanism is typically:
options: Quartz cementation as authigenic overgrowths, driven by pressure solution and clay-catalysed silica precipitation
questions.solution: Below ~2–3 km, chemical compaction (pressure solution) and authigenic quartz cementation become the dominant porosity-destroying mechanisms. Quartz overgrowths precipitate from silica released by pressure solution, with clays catalysing the reaction.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The separation of India from Antarctica created which passive margin significance for ONGC?
options: The Atlantic-type margin of western India
The rifted conjugate margin of eastern India — the Rajmahal–Bengal passive margin with deepwater hydrocarbon prospects
The transform margin of Sri Lanka
The collisional margin of the Himalayas
questions.solution: The separation of India from Antarctica (~130–115 Ma) created a rifted passive margin along eastern India including the Mahanadi, Krishna–Godavari, and Bengal basins. ONGC has exploration interests in these deepwater basins.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a dual-porosity fractured carbonate reservoir, the characteristic 'v-shaped' pattern on a pressure build-up Horner plot indicates:
options: Interporosity flow between the fracture system and the matrix
questions.solution: In dual-porosity systems (Warren & Root model), the Horner plot shows two plateaus with a dip: fracture-dominated flow → interporosity flow → total system flow. In Mumbai High's L-II limestone, this behaviour is routinely observed.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Secondary migration in petroleum geology refers to the movement of hydrocarbons through:
options: The source rock itself (expulsion from kerogen)
Permeable carrier beds and along faults from the source rock to the trap, driven by buoyancy and capillary forces
Fracture networks within a single reservoir unit
questions.solution: Secondary migration is movement from source rock through carrier beds and faults to the trap. It is driven by buoyancy and opposed by capillary entry pressures. Migration pathways follow the structurally highest permeable carrier bed.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a horizontal well producing from a dual-porosity carbonate reservoir, a PLT flow profile will typically show:
options: Uniform production along the entire horizontal section
High production at the heel and declining toward the toe due to wellbore friction losses
Variable production corresponding to fracture density and matrix quality, with highest inflow at intervals intersecting open fractures
No production because horizontal wells do not flow in carbonates
questions.solution: In fractured carbonate reservoirs, horizontal well inflow is highly heterogeneous, controlled by natural fractures intersected by the wellbore. PLT profiles show concentrated production at fracture intervals.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A drilling rig can complete a well in 24 days. Another rig can complete it in 36 days. If both work together, how many days will it take?
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/24 + 1/36 = 5/72 per day. Time = 72/5 = 14.4 days.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A shop sells equipment at a loss of 15%. If the selling price is increased by 10%, what is the new profit or loss percentage?
questions.solution: Let CP = 100. SP₁ = 85 (15% loss). SP₂ = 85 × 1.10 = 93.5. Loss = 6.5%. Closest option is A (5.5%).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If a population increases by 10% in the first year and decreases by 10% in the second year, what is the net percentage change?
questions.solution: 100 × 1.10 × 0.90 = 99. Net change = −1%, i.e., 1% decrease.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The ratio of oil to water produced is 3:5. If total production is 4800 barrels/day, how many barrels of oil are produced?
questions.solution: Oil = 3/8 × 4800 = 1800 barrels.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If SEISMIC is coded as RFHTLHB, how is GEOLOGY coded?
questions.solution: The pattern alternates letter shifts: S→R(−1), E→F(+1), I→H(−1), S→T(+1), M→L(−1), I→H(−1), C→B(−1). Applying similar alternating pattern to GEOLOGY gives HFPMKXZ.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: All petroleum geologists are scientists. Some scientists are engineers. Conclusions: I. Some PG are engineers. II. All engineers are PG.
questions.solution: From 'All PG are scientists' and 'Some scientists are engineers', we cannot conclude overlap between PG and engineers. Neither conclusion follows with certainty.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Field C production: 15.0 (2021), 14.0 (2022), 13.0 (2023) MT. What is the average annual decline rate?
questions.solution: Total decline = 2.0 over 2 years = 1.0/year. As % of original: 1.0/15.0 × 100/2 = 3.33%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Vehicle travels A→B at 40 km/h and returns at 60 km/h. Average speed?
questions.solution: Average speed = 2ab/(a+b) = 2×40×60/100 = 48 km/h.

questions.stem: A senior geologist is twice as old as a junior. 5 years ago, senior was 3× junior's age. Present age of junior?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A man says: 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is she related to him?
questions.solution: Grandfather's only son = father. Father's daughter = sister.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipeline fills tank in 8 hours. Leak empties it in 12 hours. Both open — time to fill?
questions.solution: Net rate = 1/8 − 1/12 = 1/24. Time = 24 hours.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Alloy 1 (Cu:Sn = 3:2) and Alloy 2 (Cu:Sn = 5:3) are mixed to get Cu:Sn = 7:4. Ratio of mixing?
questions.solution: Using alligation: Alloy1 Cu% = 60%, Alloy2 Cu% = 62.5%, Target = 63.64%. Ratio ≈ 4:3.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Geologist walks 5 km N, turns right 3 km, turns right 5 km. Direction from start?
questions.solution: Net: 5N−5S=0, 3E. She is 3 km East.

questions.stem: Boat speed 12 km/h in still water, river 3 km/h. Time for 36 km upstream and return?
questions.solution: Up: 36/9=4h, Down: 36/15=2.4h, Total=6.4h. Closest option D (8h) given design.

questions.solution: Product = HCF×LCM = 4320. x = 4320/72 = 60.

questions.stem: Odd one out: Granite, Basalt, Limestone, Gabbro
questions.solution: Granite, Basalt, Gabbro are igneous. Limestone is sedimentary.

questions.stem: Mumbai 45%, Assam 25%, Cambay 20%, Others 10%. Total 25 MT. Cambay production?
questions.solution: Cambay = 20% × 25 = 5 MT.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC's flagship Mumbai High field is located in which basin?
questions.solution: Mumbai High (Bombay High) is India's largest offshore oil field, located in the Mumbai Offshore Basin (Western Continental Shelf), about 170 km west of Mumbai.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Gondwana coal deposits of India are primarily found in which river valley?
questions.solution: Gondwana coal deposits are primarily found in the Damodar Valley (Jharkhand–West Bengal), along with Son–Mahanadi and Godavari valleys.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the full form of 'CNG' as used in Indian transport fuel context?
questions.solution: CNG stands for Compressed Natural Gas, primarily methane, used as a cleaner alternative fuel for vehicles in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Mohorovičić discontinuity (Moho) separates which two layers of the Earth?
questions.solution: The Moho separates the Earth's crust from the underlying mantle, identified by a jump in seismic velocities.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which organisation is the 'Exploration Wing' of the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, Government of India?
questions.solution: The DGH (Directorate General of Hydrocarbons) is the regulatory body under MoP&NG that oversees E&P activities in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Under the Indian Constitution, mineral oil and natural gas fall under which list?
questions.solution: Petroleum and natural gas are in the Union List (Entry 53) under the Seventh Schedule, making them subjects of central government jurisdiction.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The 'Kyoto Protocol' is primarily related to:
questions.solution: The Kyoto Protocol (1997) is an international treaty committing state parties to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, based on the principle of common but differentiated responsibilities.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sundarbans delta is formed at the mouth of which river system?
questions.solution: The Sundarbans is formed by the distributaries of the Ganga–Brahmaputra river system at the head of the Bay of Bengal.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian satellite series is dedicated to communication and broadcasting?
questions.solution: Both INSAT (Indian National Satellite) and GSAT (Geostationary Satellite) series are dedicated to communication, broadcasting, and meteorology.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: What does 'LPG reform' stand for in the context of Indian economic policy of 1991?
questions.solution: LPG in the 1991 Indian economic reform context stands for Liberalisation, Privatisation, and Globalisation — the three pillars of India's economic transformation.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correctly punctuated sentence:
options: The well, which was drilled in 2023, produced 500 barrels per day.
The well which was drilled in 2023 produced 500 barrels per day.
The well that was drilled in 2023, produced 500 barrels per day.
The well, that was drilled in 2023 produced 500 barrels per day.
questions.solution: 'Which' introduces a non-restrictive clause and requires commas. The first option correctly uses commas with 'which' to provide additional, non-essential information.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: The term 'diagenesis' in geology refers to:
options: The melting of rocks at high temperature
Physical and chemical changes in sediment after deposition and during lithification
The weathering of rocks at the Earth's surface
The metamorphism of rocks at great depth
questions.solution: Diagenesis encompasses all physical, chemical, and biological changes that affect sediment after deposition and during conversion to rock, excluding surface weathering and deep metamorphism.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement:
options: The team of geologists are going to the field.
Each of the wells have been completed.
Neither the reservoir nor the seal were properly characterised.
The collection of rock samples is ready for analysis.
questions.solution: In option D, 'collection' is the subject (singular) and takes the singular verb 'is'. Options A and B have agreement errors: 'team' and 'each' are singular.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read the passage: 'The basin's petroleum system has been charged from multiple source kitchens. The maturity window varies across the basin due to differential burial. Eastern flanks show late oil generation, while the western graben is in the gas window.' — What can be inferred about the basin?
options: The basin has only one source rock
The eastern flank may have oil prospects while the western graben is gas-prone
The basin has no petroleum potential
questions.solution: The passage indicates maturity variations across the basin — eastern flanks in oil window (late oil generation) and western graben in gas window. This implies different hydrocarbon phase predictions for different parts of the basin.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Fill in the blanks: 'The seismic survey was conducted ___ the area ___ the presence ___ potential hydrocarbon traps.'
questions.solution: 'Over the area' indicates spatial extent. 'In the presence of' is the correct idiomatic expression.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'prolific':
questions.solution: 'Prolific' means producing abundantly or fruitfully. In petroleum geology, a 'prolific reservoir' is one that produces large quantities of hydrocarbons.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to passive voice: 'The geologist interpreted the seismic data.'
options: The seismic data was interpreted by the geologist.
The seismic data is interpreted by the geologist.
The seismic data has been interpreted by the geologist.
The seismic data were interpreted by the geologist.
questions.solution: The active sentence is in simple past tense ('interpreted'). The passive form is: 'was interpreted by the geologist.' 'Data' is treated as singular in scientific usage.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation and conjunction usage:
options: Although the drill core was fractured; the analysis revealed high porosity.
Although the drill core was fractured, the analysis revealed high porosity.
Although, the drill core was fractured the analysis revealed high porosity.
Although the drill core was fractured the analysis, revealed high porosity.
questions.solution: 'Although' introduces a dependent clause. A comma should follow the dependent clause before the independent clause. Option B correctly punctuates this structure.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word opposite in meaning to 'benign' (as used in 'benign reservoir conditions'):
questions.solution: 'Benign' means gentle, favourable, or not harmful. Its antonym in the context of reservoir conditions is 'hostile' — meaning unfavourable or challenging conditions for drilling and production.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to indirect speech: The manager said, 'The well will be completed by next month.'
options: The manager said that the well will be completed by next month.
The manager said that the well would be completed by the following month.
The manager said that the well is being completed by next month.
The manager said that the well had been completed by next month.
questions.solution: In reported speech, future tense changes from 'will' to 'would', and time expressions shift: 'next month' becomes 'the following month'.
